title: centaurea calcitrapa
intro: Centaurea calcitrapa, also known as caltrop or purple cockle, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's flowers and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. Centaurea calcitrapa has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
